# 7.0 Cochrane Masters — All-115 Performance Audit Spec Companion

**Source file (verbatim, do not edit):**
- `src/master/knowledge/source-documents/brands/cochrane-master-builders/strategy/7.0_cochrane_masters_all_115_performance_audit_specs.xlsx`
- `…/7.0_cochrane_masters_all_115_performance_audit_specs.source.md` (1:1 markdown mirror)

## Category
brands → cochrane-master-builders → strategy → per-site **performance posture + Core Web Vitals audit spec** (sixth file in the paired family-of-sites set).

## Main purpose
Canonical per-site **performance posture** and **audit spec** for every one of the 115 Cochrane Master Builders spin-off domains. Provides the "speed, weight, and Core Web Vitals" leg of the remix hexapod:

- **v1.0** — SEO / positioning
- **v2.0** — services / pricing / compliance
- **v4.0** — ICP brain
- **v5.0** — color / UX direction
- **v6.0** — UX experience temperament
- **v7.0 (this file)** — performance audit spec / specific optimization

All six are joined on `Master Row`. Any per-site decision must consult all six together.

## What this document should influence
- Per-site **performance budgets**: LCP / INP / CLS targets, JS/CSS/image weight ceilings, TTFB stance.
- **Asset strategy**: image format (AVIF/WebP), responsive crops, lazy-load rules, font subsetting, icon strategy.
- **Render strategy**: above-the-fold simplicity, hydration discipline, deferred third-party scripts, render-blocking elimination.
- **Mobile-first posture**: 4G budget, touch latency, network-aware behavior, reduced-motion fallbacks.
- **Trust + speed pairing**: never trade away the trust / legitimacy posture set by v4.0 and v6.0 to chase a metric.
- **Audit checklists** Auditor mode runs before any spin-off ships.

## Triggers — when to consult
*performance, speed, Core Web Vitals, CWV, LCP, INP, CLS, TTFB, page weight, bundle size, image optimization, AVIF, WebP, lazy load, font loading, FOUT/FOIT, hydration, third-party scripts, render blocking, perf budget, audit, lighthouse, mobile speed, 4G, network-aware, perf checklist, trust + speed.*

## Adaptation rules / firewall
- Brand context is **Cochrane Master Builders** only. No automotive vocabulary firewall (this is the construction brand family, not VeePo / Masters Detailing).
- These are **directional posture specs**, not finished perf budgets. Reconcile against the cross-brand **React 18 + Vite Performance Engineer** persona and the locked memories (Hero Section Lock, Motion Philosophy, Loading Sequence, Cloth Wipe, Parallax Coverage Specs, Booking Modal Architecture) before shipping any actual budget, config, or code.
- **Hard rule (inherited from the Performance Engineer persona):** never alter design, layout, motion, copy, or hierarchy as a side-effect of performance work. Perf serves the design — not the other way around.
- **Per-site only.** Never blend rows. Always look up by `Master Row`.
- **Join discipline.** Any per-site work must reference the same `Master Row` across all six files. Drift in `Recommended Domain` between any two files = **Sync Discrepancy** (flag, do not silently reconcile).
- **Backend intelligence only.** Embedding does not change any front-end output. The current VeePo / Masters Detailing front-end stays untouched and no Cochrane spin-off site exists in this codebase yet.

## Mode-OS pairing
- **Architect** — uses the row's perf spec when scaffolding a new spin-off's perf budget, asset strategy, and render plan.
- **Mapper** — can render per-cluster perf-posture matrices or per-site critical-render-path flows.
- **Auditor** — pressure-tests built sites against (a) the row's perf spec and (b) the cross-brand Performance Engineer's 11-section playbook. Failing the row's stated posture = **High** bug; failing CWV thresholds = **Critical**.

## Dependencies
**Paired set (consult together):** v1.0 · v2.0 · v4.0 · v5.0 · v6.0 · **v7.0 (this file)**.

**Cross-brand personas:** React 18 + Vite Performance Engineer; Premium Scroll Animation (motion budget); Mobile Wrapping (4G + touch budget); Image SEO + Local Visibility (image format / sitemap); the 3-mode OS personas (Architect / Mapper / Auditor).

**Cochrane brand-identity:** `1.2.1` Family Legacy Standard, `1.2.2` Foundations. **Cochrane UX:** `1.3.1` Bespoke / Traditional UX Design Phase Report.

## Guidance type
Brand-global for **Cochrane Master Builders** only.

## Practical examples
- *"What's the perf budget for the Basement Suite Masters site?"* → Pull the row's posture from v7.0; reconcile with the Performance Engineer 11-section playbook.
- *"Audit this spin-off's Core Web Vitals."* → Compare measured LCP / INP / CLS vs. the row's stated spec; classify gaps via Auditor severity rules above.
- *"Should we lazy-load the hero image?"* → Hero Section Lock + the row's render strategy decide; Performance Engineer enforces the no-design-side-effect rule.

## Out of scope
No frontend changes. No edits to the `.xlsx`. The `.source.md` is a 1:1 cell mirror — no synthesis. No actual perf budgets, configs, or code shipped from this embed; it only powers future perf decisions.
